title: Allophylus integrifolius
taxonomy: Allophylus integrifolius is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It belongs to the genus Allophylus, which comprises approximately 400 species of trees and shrubs. This plant is native to tropical Africa, specifically in countries such as the Democratic Republic of Congo, Angola, and Mozambique. The classification of Allophylus integrifolius is further supported by its botanical name, which includes the specific epithet "integrifolius" indicating its leaves are entire and lack lobes. Allophylus integrifolius is often found in tropical forests and woodlands, where it thrives in well-drained soils and full sun to partial shade conditions.
uses: Allophylus integrifolius is a plant species with reported medicinal uses. The plant is traditionally used in West African countries to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. Its leaves and roots are used in traditional medicine to create remedies for these conditions. The plant's antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential health benefits and confirm its efficacy in treating specific conditions.
